_doc: Punta Cana press & backlink outreach — consumed by the shared sender script (pending: scripts/automation/press-outreach.cjs, Resend, same domain protection as the drip: batches + stop if bounce rate > 8%). Tokens resolved at SEND time from public/api/copernicus/puntacana/sargassum.json — never invented: {cleanToday}=count of levels with status==='clean', {beachCount}=12, {todayDate}=long date, {pressUrl}, {mapUrl}, {outletName}, {contactName} (fallback: greeting without name). contactEmail is null where unknown — the sender script discovers it (contact page / press@ pattern probe) and must NEVER guess-send to an unverified address.
guardrails: NEVER use the angle 'institutional sources are weekly, we are daily' — NOAA SIR is daily since v1.5; the angle is dead and discrediting.
ALWAYS keep the honesty pattern: days 1-3 reliable, days 4-7 trend.

NEVER invent numbers. The only figures allowed are tokens resolved from live product data, the published backtest percentages, and the widely reported ~28.9M tonnes season figure attributed as 'widely reported'.
Partnership tone with bloggers (complement their content, serve their readers), never 'replace your recommendation'.
One follow-up maximum, then stop. Unsubscribe/never-contact honored permanently in the sender log.

targets.rationale: Top organic authority for Punta Cana travel queries; its sargassum guide is updated roughly monthly and sends readers to Facebook groups for the daily picture. A live per-beach status link/embed is a reader service it cannot build itself.
English-language Dominican Republic news outlet; covers sargassum and tourism regularly. Angle: record Atlantic season translated to which Punta Cana beach is clean today.
Major Dominican national daily (Spanish). Angle: temporada récord de sargazo — dato satelital playa por playa en Punta Cana/Bávaro, gratis para citar.
Major Dominican national daily (Spanish). Same angle as Diario Libre; tourism section covers Bávaro/Punta Cana beach conditions.
Long-running English-language DR news digest read by expats and frequent visitors; links out to useful tools.
Caribbean travel trade outlet; writes sargassum season stories across the region. Angle: a per-beach, per-day satellite answer for the most-booked destination in the Caribbean.
US travel trade/consumer outlet that has covered the record sargassum season without a beach-level tool to point readers to.

partnershipBlogger.subject: A live per-beach sargassum status for your Punta Cana readers
partnershipBlogger.body:
Hi {contactName},

Your sargassum guide is the best-researched page on the subject for Punta Cana — it is where the search traffic deservedly goes. The one thing a monthly update cannot give readers is today's picture, which is why so many of them end up digging through Facebook groups.

We built that missing piece: a free live map of {beachCount} Punta Cana beaches (Bávaro, Macao, Cap Cana…), each with a clean/moderate/avoid status and a 7-day forecast, from Copernicus/NOAA satellite data refreshed four times a day. Right now it shows {cleanToday} of {beachCount} beaches clean. Days 1-3 of the forecast are the most reliable; days 4-7 show the trend — we publish our measured accuracy openly.

If it would serve your readers, you are welcome to link it from your guide or embed the live status — no cost, no catch, attribution is all we ask. Press page with details: {pressUrl}

Happy to answer anything.

Best,
The SargassumPuntaCana team
{mapUrl}

pressOutlet.subject: Record sargassum season: which Punta Cana beach is actually clean today (free per-beach satellite data)
pressOutlet.body:
Hi {contactName},

This season's Atlantic sargassum bloom is widely reported at record levels (~28.9 million tonnes). The coverage gap: none of it tells readers what the bloom means at beach level — whether Bávaro is swimmable this Saturday while Macao is buried.

We publish exactly that for Punta Cana: live status for {beachCount} beaches with a 0-100 score and a 7-day forecast per beach, from Copernicus/NOAA AFAI satellite data refreshed four times a day. As of {todayDate}, {cleanToday} of {beachCount} tracked beaches read clean. Our forecast backtests are published openly (next-day status verifies 86% of the time across our Caribbean network; days 4-7 are trend only).

Everything is free to cite with a link, and we can provide beach-level data or comment for a story: {pressUrl}

Best regards,
The SargassumPuntaCana team
{mapUrl}

pressOutletEs.subject: Temporada récord de sargazo: qué playa de Punta Cana está limpia hoy (dato satelital por playa, gratis para citar)
pressOutletEs.body:
Hola {contactName}:

La temporada de sargazo en el Atlántico se reporta ampliamente como récord (~28,9 millones de toneladas). Lo que falta en la cobertura es el dato a nivel de playa: si Bávaro está nadable este sábado mientras Macao amanece cubierta.

Publicamos exactamente eso para Punta Cana: estado en vivo de {beachCount} playas con puntuación 0-100 y pronóstico de 7 días por playa, a partir de datos satelitales AFAI de Copernicus/NOAA actualizados cuatro veces al día. Hoy, {todayDate}, {cleanToday} de {beachCount} playas monitoreadas están limpias. La precisión del pronóstico está publicada abiertamente (el estado a 1 día se verifica el 86% de las veces en nuestra red del Caribe; los días 4-7 son tendencia).

Todo es gratis para citar con enlace, y podemos aportar datos por playa o declaraciones para una nota: {pressUrl}

Saludos cordiales,
El equipo de SargassumPuntaCana
{mapUrl}
